text: The `cusip` module's `is_valid` function returns false for a CUSIP that I would expect would pass, as I found it on a real Fidelity statement (see screenshot of document sample). ``` cusip.is_valid(`FDIC99425`) >> False ``` I believe this is because `cusip._alphabet` does not permit "I" or "O": ``` cusip._alphabet >> '0123456789ABCDEFGH JKLMN PQRSTUVWXYZ*@#' ``` I checked [cusip.com](https://www.cusip.com/identifiers.html#/CUSIP) and I didn't find anything indicating that those two characters were not allowed.
Thanks for pointing this out. It's been fixed in 1364e19

Support "I" and "O" in CUSIP number

It is unclear why these letters were considered invalid at the time of
the implementation.

This also reduces the test set a bit while still covering most cases.

Closes https://github.com/arthurdejong/python-stdnum/issues/337
